context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements a dual-pool database access layer in TypeScript with appropriate connection limits, correct read/write routing including the cart-to-primary rule, and proper transaction support.

checklist.name: Primary pool max connections
checklist.description: The primary database pool is configured with max: 20 (not the original 10 or another value)

checklist.name: Replica pool max connections
checklist.description: The replica database pool is configured with max: 50 (higher than the primary)

checklist.name: Separate pool instances
checklist.description: Two distinct Pool instances are created — one using DATABASE_URL (primary) and one using DATABASE_REPLICA_URL (or equivalent replica env var)

checklist.name: Transaction method on primary
checklist.description: A transaction method exists that acquires a client from the primary pool, executes BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK, and calls client.release()

checklist.name: queryRead routes to replica
checklist.description: A read method (queryRead or equivalent) issues queries against the replica pool, not the primary pool

checklist.name: queryWrite routes to primary
checklist.description: A write method (queryWrite or equivalent) issues queries against the primary pool

checklist.name: Cart reads on primary
checklist.description: The README or code comments explicitly state that cart reads must use the primary (not replica) to avoid stale data from replication lag

checklist.name: Catalog reads on replica
checklist.description: The README documents that product listing / catalog queries should use the read method routed to the replica

checklist.name: Order history on replica
checklist.description: The README documents that order history reads (where slight staleness is acceptable) should use the replica read method

checklist.name: pg package used
checklist.description: The implementation uses the 'pg' package (Pool from 'pg') rather than an ORM or unrelated database library
